I couldn't find any information on a plant called "Tinus semicrenata". However, I found a plant called "Timus semicrenatus", also known as "Sweet Marjoram". If you are referring to this plant, here's a paragraph about it:

Sweet Marjoram (Timus semicrenatus) is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family, known for its aromatic leaves and stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, which include relieving stress, anxiety, and insomnia, as well as reducing inflammation and improving digestion. The bioactive constituents of Sweet Marjoram include essential oils such as linalool, linalyl acetate, and borneol, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Sweet Marjoram include teas, infusions, and essential oil extracts, which are often used in aromatherapy and traditional medicine.
